questions.stem: What is the sum of all solutions to \(|2x - 3| = 7\)?
questions.explanation:
An absolute value equation has two cases.
$$\begin{aligned}2x - 3 &= 7 & 2x - 3 &= -7 \\ 2x &= 10 & 2x &= -4 \\ x &= 5 & x &= -2\end{aligned}$$
The sum of all solutions is \(5 + (-2) = 3\).

questions.stem: For what value of \(k\) does the equation \(3x + 6 = 3(x + k)\) have infinitely many solutions?
questions.explanation:
Expand the right side: \(3x + 6 = 3x + 3k\). Subtract \(3x\) from both sides: \(6 = 3k\). For infinitely many solutions, this must be true for all values of \(x\) — which means the equation must be an identity.
$$6 = 3k \implies k = 2$$
When \(k = 2\), both sides are identical, giving infinitely many solutions. When \(k \neq 2\), the equation has no solution.

questions.stem:
$$2x + 4y = 8$$
$$kx + 2y = 3$$
For what value of \(k\) does the system of equations above have no solution?
questions.explanation:
A system has no solution when the lines are parallel — same slope but different \(y\)-intercepts. Write each equation in slope-intercept form.
$$\begin{aligned}2x + 4y &= 8 \implies y = -\tfrac{1}{2}x + 2 \\ kx + 2y &= 3 \implies y = -\tfrac{k}{2}x + \tfrac{3}{2}\end{aligned}$$
Set slopes equal: \(-\frac{1}{2} = -\frac{k}{2} \implies k = 1\). Check: the \(y\)-intercepts are 2 and \(\frac{3}{2}\), which are different, confirming no solution.

questions.stem: Which of the following is equivalent to \(\dfrac{x^2 - 9}{x - 3}\) for \(x \neq 3\)?
questions.explanation:
Factor the numerator as a difference of squares.
$$\frac{x^2 - 9}{x - 3} = \frac{(x+3)(x-3)}{x-3} = x + 3$$
The factor \((x - 3)\) cancels, leaving \(x + 3\). Note that \(x \neq 3\) because the original expression is undefined at \(x = 3\).

questions.stem: Which of the following represents all values of \(x\) that satisfy \(x^2 - 5x + 6 < 0\)?
questions.explanation:
Factor the quadratic: \(x^2 - 5x + 6 = (x-2)(x-3)\). The expression equals zero at \(x = 2\) and \(x = 3\), and we need it to be less than zero.
Test a value between the roots, say \(x = 2.5\): \((0.5)(-0.5) = -0.25 < 0\) ✓. The product is negative between the roots.
$$2 < x < 3$$

questions.stem: Which of the following is equivalent to \((3 + 2i)(1 - 4i)\)?
questions.explanation:
Expand using FOIL and use the fact that \(i^2 = -1\).
$$\begin{aligned}(3 + 2i)(1 - 4i) &= 3 - 12i + 2i - 8i^2 \\ &= 3 - 10i - 8(-1) \\ &= 3 - 10i + 8 \\ &= 11 - 10i\end{aligned}$$

questions.stem: The function \(f\) is linear. If \(f(2) = 7\) and \(f(5) = 16\), what is \(f(0)\)?
questions.explanation:
Find the slope using the two given points.
$$m = \frac{16 - 7}{5 - 2} = \frac{9}{3} = 3$$
Use point-slope form to find the \(y\)-intercept: \(f(x) = 3x + b\). Substitute \(f(2) = 7\): \(7 = 6 + b \implies b = 1\). Therefore \(f(0) = 3(0) + 1 = 1\).

questions.stem:
$$y = x^2 - 4$$
$$y = 2x - 1$$
The system of equations above has two solutions \((x_1, y_1)\) and \((x_2, y_2)\). What is the value of \(x_1 + x_2\)?
questions.explanation:
Set the expressions equal and solve.
$$\begin{aligned}x^2 - 4 &= 2x - 1 \\ x^2 - 2x - 3 &= 0 \\ (x-3)(x+1) &= 0\end{aligned}$$
The solutions are \(x = 3\) and \(x = -1\). Therefore \(x_1 + x_2 = 3 + (-1) = 2\).

questions.stem: When \(p(x) = x^3 - 2x^2 + 3x - 4\) is divided by \((x - 2)\), what is the remainder?
questions.explanation:
By the Remainder Theorem, the remainder when \(p(x)\) is divided by \((x - 2)\) is \(p(2)\).
$$p(2) = (2)^3 - 2(2)^2 + 3(2) - 4 = 8 - 8 + 6 - 4 = 2$$
The remainder is \(2\). Note that since the remainder is not 0, \(x = 2\) is not a root of \(p(x)\).

questions.stem: Which values of \(x\) are NOT in the domain of \(f(x) = \dfrac{x + 2}{x^2 - x - 6}\)?
questions.explanation:
A rational function is undefined where the denominator equals zero. Factor the denominator.
$$x^2 - x - 6 = (x - 3)(x + 2) = 0 \implies x = 3 \text{ or } x = -2$$
Both values make the denominator zero and are excluded from the domain. Note that \(x = -2\) also makes the numerator zero, but it is still excluded — the function has a hole there, not a defined value.

questions.stem: The function \(f\) is defined by \(f(x) = x^2 - 8x + 7\). What is the minimum value of \(f(x)\)?
questions.explanation:
Complete the square to write the function in vertex form.
$$\begin{aligned}f(x) &= x^2 - 8x + 7 \\ &= x^2 - 8x + 16 - 16 + 7 \\ &= (x - 4)^2 - 9\end{aligned}$$
The vertex is at \((4, -9)\). Since the parabola opens upward (\(a = 1 > 0\)), the minimum value is \(-9\).

questions.stem: What is the value of \(x\) in the equation \(4^x = 8^{x-1}\)?
questions.explanation:
Write both sides as powers of 2.
$$4^x = 8^{x-1} \implies 2^{2x} = 2^{3(x-1)}$$
Set the exponents equal and solve.
$$\begin{aligned}2x &= 3(x-1) \\ 2x &= 3x - 3 \\ x &= 3\end{aligned}$$

questions.stem: The function \(f\) is defined by \(f(x) = 2x + 6\). Which of the following defines \(f^{-1}(x)\)?
questions.explanation:
To find the inverse, replace \(f(x)\) with \(y\), swap \(x\) and \(y\), then solve for \(y\).
$$\begin{aligned}y &= 2x + 6 \\ x &= 2y + 6 \\ x - 6 &= 2y \\ y &= \frac{x-6}{2}\end{aligned}$$

questions.stem: If \((x - k)\) is a factor of \(p(x) = x^3 - 7x + 6\), which of the following could be a value of \(k\)?
questions.explanation:
By the Factor Theorem, \((x - k)\) is a factor of \(p(x)\) if and only if \(p(k) = 0\). Test each choice.
$$ egin{aligned}p(4) &= 64 - 28 + 6 = 42 eq 0 \ p(-1) &= -1 + 7 + 6 = 12 eq 0 \ p(2) &= 8 - 14 + 6 = 0 \checkmark \ p(5) &= 125 - 35 + 6 = 96 eq 0\end{aligned}$$

questions.stem: What are the solutions to \(x^2 + 4x + 13 = 0\)?
questions.explanation:
Use the quadratic formula with \(a = 1\), \(b = 4\), \(c = 13\).
$$x = \frac{-4 \pm \sqrt{16 - 52}}{2} = \frac{-4 \pm \sqrt{-36}}{2} = \frac{-4 \pm 6i}{2} = -2 \pm 3i$$

questions.stem: A population doubles every 12 years. If the initial population is 800, after how many years will the population first reach 3,200?
questions.explanation:
Set up an exponential equation where the population doubles every 12 years.
$$3{,}200 = 800 \cdot 2^{t/12}$$
Divide both sides by 800, then solve for \(t\).
$$\begin{aligned}4 &= 2^{t/12} \\ 2^2 &= 2^{t/12} \\ 2 &= \frac{t}{12} \\ t &= 24\end{aligned}$$

questions.stem: Two classes take the same test. Class A has a mean score of 75 and a standard deviation of 3. Class B has a mean score of 75 and a standard deviation of 12. Which of the following statements is true?
choices: Class A scored higher on average than Class B.
Class B scored higher on average than Class A.
Class A has more variation in scores than Class B.
Class B has more variation in scores than Class A.
questions.explanation:
Both classes have the same mean (75), so neither scored higher on average. Standard deviation measures the spread of data around the mean. A larger standard deviation indicates more variation.
Class B has a standard deviation of 12, which is larger than Class A's standard deviation of 3. Therefore Class B has more variation in scores.

questions.stem: In a group of 50 students, 30 play sports and 20 do not. Of those who play sports, 18 passed an exam. Of those who do not play sports, 10 passed. What is the probability that a randomly selected student who passed the exam plays sports?
questions.explanation:
This is a conditional probability question. Find the total number of students who passed, then find the fraction of those who play sports.
$$\text{Total passed} = 18 + 10 = 28$$
$$P(\text{sports} \mid \text{passed}) = \frac{18}{28} = \frac{9}{14}$$

questions.stem: A researcher wants to determine the average number of hours teenagers in a city spend on social media per day. She surveys 200 teenagers at a local shopping mall. Which of the following best describes a limitation of this method?
choices: The sample size of 200 is too small to draw any conclusions.
Teenagers at a shopping mall may not be representative of all teenagers in the city.
The researcher should have surveyed adults as well as teenagers.
Social media usage cannot be accurately measured through self-reporting.
questions.explanation: A key principle of sampling is that the sample should be representative of the population. Teenagers at a shopping mall are a specific subset — they may have different social media habits, schedules, or demographics than teenagers who are not at the mall at that time. This introduces sampling bias.

questions.stem: A line of best fit for data comparing years of work experience \((x)\) and annual salary in thousands of dollars \((y)\) has the equation \(y = 3.2x + 42\). What does \(3.2\) represent in this context?
choices: The predicted starting salary in thousands of dollars with no experience
The number of years needed to reach a salary of \$42,000
The predicted increase in annual salary, in thousands of dollars, for each additional year of experience
The total salary in thousands of dollars after 3.2 years
questions.explanation:
In the equation \(y = 3.2x + 42\), the coefficient \(3.2\) is the slope. In context, the slope represents the rate of change — for each additional year of experience (increase of 1 in \(x\)), the predicted salary increases by 3.2 thousand dollars, or \$3,200.
The constant 42 represents the \(y\)-intercept — the predicted starting salary of \$42,000 with zero years of experience.

questions.stem: A circle has a radius of 9. A sector of the circle has a central angle of \(80°\). What is the area of the sector?
questions.explanation:
The area of a sector is a fraction of the total circle area, determined by the central angle.
$$\text{Area of sector} = \frac{80}{360} \times \pi r^2 = \frac{2}{9} \times \pi(9)^2 = \frac{2}{9} \times 81\pi = 18\pi$$

questions.stem: In triangle \(ABC\), \(AB = 7\), \(BC = 9\), and angle \(B = 60°\). What is \(AC^2\)?
questions.explanation:
Apply the Law of Cosines: \(AC^2 = AB^2 + BC^2 - 2 \cdot AB \cdot BC \cdot \cos(B)\).
$$\begin{aligned}AC^2 &= 7^2 + 9^2 - 2(7)(9)\cos(60°) \\ &= 49 + 81 - 126 \cdot \frac{1}{2} \\ &= 130 - 63 \\ &= 67\end{aligned}$$
